parameters.value:
import json
import re

# Shuffle injecte les sorties Python comme littéraux Python avec true/false/null JSON
true  = True
false = False
null  = None

# ── Stacks de mots-clés ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STACK_URGENT = [
    # Fortinet
    "fortinet","fortigate","fortios","fortiproxy","forticlient","fortimanager",
    "fortianalyzer","fortisiem","fortiweb","fortimail","fortiedr","fortisoar",
    "fortinac","fortiswitch","fortiap","forticloud","fortiddos","fortiauth",
    "fortitoken","fortindr",
    # Stormshield
    "stormshield","stormshield network security","stormshield endpoint security",
    "stormshield management center","ses","sna","smc",
    # OPNsense / pfSense
    "opnsense","pfsense",
    # Wazuh / SIEM stack
    "wazuh","opensearch","kibana","logstash","filebeat",
    # Shuffle / SOAR
    "shuffle","soar",
    # Threat Intel / DFIR
    "opencti","misp","cortex","dfir-iris","velociraptor","thehive",
    # Active Directory & Windows critiques
    "active directory","kerberos","ntlm","lsass","dcsync","ldap","ldaps","gpo",
    "group policy","domain controller","ad cs","adcs","certifried","zerologon",
    "printnightmare","petitpotam","pass the hash","pass the ticket","golden ticket",
    "silver ticket","asreproasting","kerberoasting","ntlm relay","smb relay",
    "relay attack","dcshadow","skeleton key","sid history",
    # Windows Server
    "windows server 2022","windows server 2025","windows server 2019","windows server 2016",
    # Windows 11/10 & produits Microsoft critiques
    "windows 11","windows 10","bitlocker","windows defender","microsoft defender",
    "azure ad","entra id","entra","sharepoint","teams","exchange server",
    # SD-WAN / Cisco critique
    "sd-wan","sdwan","catalyst sd-wan","cisco secure workload", "Cisco ASA", "Cisco FTD", "Cisco identity services engine software",
    # Backup ciblés par ransomware
    "veeam","commvault","rubrik","nakivo","acronis","cohesity",
    # Exploits critiques génériques
    "zero-day","0-day","rce","remote code execution","privilege escalation",
    "unauthenticated","pre-auth","authentication bypass","arbitrary code",
    "critical","cvss 9","cvss 10","max severity","maximum severity",
    "actively exploited","exploit in the wild","exploited in attacks",
    "weaponized","proof-of-concept available","poc available",
    "sql injection","command injection","code injection","xxe","ssrf",
    # Ransomware — groupes actifs
    "ransomware","lockbit","alphv","blackcat","clop","akira","play","medusa",
    "black basta","royal","scattered spider","rhysida","hunters international",
    "meow","fog","lynx","qilin","ransomhub","eldorado","interlock","sarcoma",
    "nitrogen","embargo","killsec","incransom","dragonforce","darkangels",
    # APT / Nation state
    "nation state","apt","lazarus","apt28","apt29","apt41","sandworm",
    "cozy bear","fancy bear","volt typhoon","salt typhoon","muddywater",
    "charming kitten","kimsuky","turla","fin7","ta505",
    # Santé / HDS spécifique
    "healthcare","hospital","hôpital","santé","hds","hl7","dicom","fhir","pacs",
    "scada santé","médical","medical device","ehpad","chu","chru","ch ",
    "ans ","agence du numérique en santé","segur numerique","mediboard",
    "crossway","orbis","hexagone","hopital numerique","enrs","coreye",
    # Menaces critiques / TTPs
    "supply chain attack","living off the land","lolbin","lolbas","lotl",
    "credential dumping","mimikatz","cobalt strike","brute ratel","sliver",
    "metasploit","impacket","bloodhound","sharphound","rubeus","responder",
    "covenant","havoc","nighthawk","latrodectus","bumblebee","qakbot",
    # Réglementaire critique pour HDS
    "violation de données","data breach","fuite de données","compromission",
    "cyberattaque","intrusion","compromis",
]

STACK_NORMAL = [
    # Web / applicatif
    "apache","nginx","iis","tomcat","php","wordpress","drupal","joomla",
    "openssl","tls","ssl","http/2","websocket","log4j","log4shell","spring",
    "struts","jenkins","gitlab","github","bitbucket","sonarqube","nexus",
    "artifactory","activemq","rabbitmq","kafka",
    # Accès distant
    "ssh","rdp","vnc","telnet","citrix","anydesk","teamviewer","pulse secure",
    "globalprotect","openvpn","wireguard","ipsec","radius","nac",
    # Protocoles réseau
    "smb","nfs","ftp","sftp","snmp","dns","bgp","ospf","ldap","dhcp","ntp",
    "dmarc","spf","dkim","smtp","imap","pop3",
    # Messagerie / collaboration
    "exchange","outlook","postfix","sendmail","zimbra","mattermost",
    "teams","slack","zoom","skype",
    # Virtualisation / cloud
    "vmware","vsphere","vcenter","esxi","hyper-v","proxmox","xen","kvm","qemu",
    "kubernetes","k8s","docker","containerd","helm","harbor","trivy","podman",
    "aws","azure","gcp","oci","openstack","terraform","ansible","puppet","chef",
    # Linux — distributions & composants
    "ubuntu","debian","red hat","rhel","centos","alma linux","almalinux",
    "rocky linux","rocky","fedora","opensuse","suse","arch linux","kali",
    "linux kernel","glibc","sudo","polkit","systemd","pam","selinux",
    "apparmor","ufw","iptables","nftables",
    # Bases de données
    "mysql","mariadb","postgresql","postgres","mongodb","redis","memcached",
    "elasticsearch","cassandra","couchdb","influxdb","oracle database","mssql",
    "sql server","sqlite",
    # Réseau / sécurité périmétrique
    "cisco","juniper","palo alto","checkpoint","sonicwall","watchguard",
    "barracuda","f5","bigip","firewall","vpn","ids","ips","waf","utm",
    "haproxy","traefik","squid","bind9","powerdns","nftables",
    # Monitoring / ITSM
    "zabbix","nagios","prometheus","grafana","centreon","datadog","prtg",
    "glpi","manageengine","opmanager","dynatrace","newrelic","elastic apm",
    # Identité & accès
    "saml","oauth","openid","keycloak","freeipa","samba","ldaps",
    "mfa","totp","webauthn","passkey","sso","pam","hashicorp vault",
    "cyberark","beyond trust","delinea",
    # Malware générique
    "malware","backdoor","trojan","loader","dropper","stealer","infostealer",
    "rat","botnet","rootkit","keylogger","spyware","adware","wiper",
    "cryptominer","coinminer","web shell","webshell","stager","beacon",
    # Attaques
    "phishing","spearphishing","vishing","smishing","credential","brute force",
    "password spray","lateral movement","pivoting","exfiltration",
    "command and control","c2","c&c","beaconing","data exfiltration",
    "privilege","reconnaissance","enumeration","persistence","evasion",
    # CVE / vulnérabilités (générique)
    "cve-","vulnerability","vulnerabilities","vuln","patch tuesday","advisory",
    "exploit","poc","proof of concept","security update","hotfix","workaround",
    "security advisory","patch","mitigation","security fix","bulletin",
    # Divers
    "malicious","attacker","threat actor","threat group","breach","attack",
    "pwned","leaked","exposed","misconfigured","misconfiguration","insecure",
]

# ── Logique de classification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

def text_has(text_lower, keywords):
    for kw in keywords:
        if kw in text_lower:
            return True
    return False

def classify(item):
    source = item.get('source', '')
    text   = (item.get('title','') + ' ' + item.get('summary','')).lower()
    cves   = item.get('cves', [])
    in_kev = item.get('in_kev', False)

    has_urgent = text_has(text, STACK_URGENT)
    has_normal = text_has(text, STACK_NORMAL)

    if source == 'CISA KEV':
        return 'urgent'
    if source == 'CERT-FR':
        return 'urgent' if (has_urgent or has_normal) else None
    if source == 'BleepingComputer':
        if has_urgent:
            return 'urgent'
        if has_normal:
            return 'veille'
        return None
    # DFIR Report / Cisco Talos
    if has_urgent or cves:
        return 'urgent'
    if has_normal:
        return 'veille'
    return None

def sort_key_urgent(item):
    kev = 0 if item.get('in_kev') else 1
    cve = 0 if item.get('cves') else 1
    src = {'CERT-FR': 0, 'CISA KEV': 1}.get(item.get('source',''), 2)
    return (kev, cve, src)

# ── Extraction robuste depuis la sortie Shuffle ────────────────────────────────
# Shuffle encapsule la sortie du nœud précédent dans {"success":true,"message":[...]}
# raw peut donc être : dict wrapper | list directe | str JSON

raw = $parse_feeds

def extract_items(raw):
    # Cas 1 : dict wrapper Shuffle {"success":..., "message":[...]}
    if isinstance(raw, dict):
        msg = raw.get('message', [])
        if isinstance(msg, list):
            return msg
        # message peut être lui-même un dict si le print était un objet
        if isinstance(msg, dict):
            return msg.get('items', []) or []
        return []
    # Cas 2 : déjà une liste Python
    if isinstance(raw, list):
        return raw
    # Cas 3 : chaîne JSON
    if isinstance(raw, str):
        try:
            parsed = json.loads(raw)
            if isinstance(parsed, list):
                return parsed
            if isinstance(parsed, dict):
                msg = parsed.get('message', [])
                return msg if isinstance(msg, list) else []
        except Exception:
            pass
    return []

items = extract_items(raw)

urgent = []
veille = []

for item in items:
    try:
        cat = classify(item)
        if cat == 'urgent':
            urgent.append(item)
        elif cat == 'veille':
            veille.append(item)
    except Exception:
        continue

urgent.sort(key=sort_key_urgent)
urgent = urgent[:10]
veille = veille[:10]

print(json.dumps({"urgent": urgent, "veille": veille}, ensure_ascii=False))
